title: Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal
tagline: Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Tarakeshwara or Tarakeshvara Temple is a Hindu temple complex dedicated to Shiva as Tarakeswara located in Hangal, Karnataka, in India.
overview.description: The Tarakeshwara or Tarakeshvara Temple is a Hindu temple complex dedicated to Shiva as Tarakeswara located in Hangal, Karnataka, in India. It also houses a temple to Shiva's vahana, Nandi, and his son Ganesha. The complex is a listed monument of the Archaeological Survey of India. It also has three memorial stones which are sculpted with religious and military scenes and inscribed with text in the Kannada language.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (65 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal?
faq.a: Stays in Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tarakeshwara Temple, Hangal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hangal, Akkivalli, Akki Alur.
